events.description: Rose Bends' petition for sale of allotment No. 1001 was denied by Crow Indian Agency
William Bends sold 280.00 acres under a supervised sale, receiving $3,262.00 plus other smaller amounts totaling in excess of $4,000
Additional request by William Bends for supervised sale of 241.25 acres approved by agency
Peter Old Bull filed three applications for patent in fee along with completed questionnaire, sent to Senator Murray's office
BIA Billings Area Office denied Peter Old Bull's appeal from Superintendent's decision on supervised land sale
Susan Farwell Glenn's land put up for sale to highest bidder in October
Sale of 239.99 acres of allotment No. 28SA to R. Rex Roberts for $10,027 approved; patent request sent to BLM through Commissioner of Indian Affairs
Sale of 480 acres of allotment No. 28SA to Robert B. Fraser, Jr. for $15,880 approved; patent requested
Patent No. 1150460 issued to Robert B. Fraser, Jr.
Patent No. 1150457 issued to R. Rex Roberts
Patent No. 1149984 issued covering allotment No. 1545
Patent No. 1149983 issued covering allotment No. 3180
Patents No. 1149473 and No. 1149474 issued covering allotment No. 1478
Superintendent denied Lester Jefferson's application for patent in fee submitted in March 1953 covering 2 acres inherited from his father
Senator Murray referred inquiry to Bureau of Land Management regarding Lester Jefferson's desire for patent in fee
Commissioner of Indian Affairs to appear before Indian Affairs Subcommittee to explain Bureau policies on patent matters
Senator Murray sent communication to BLM enclosing correspondence from Frank Medicine Horse, Jr. and Frederick P. Knows Gun
Senator Murray sent telegram to Percy H. Glenn advising of patent numbers issued to Fraser and Roberts
Joe Morrison case sent to BLM January 7
Mr. Westwood case sent to BLM January 17

financial_transactions.description: William Bends sold 280.00 acres under supervised sale
Total received by William Bends from supervised sale plus other smaller amounts from sales of inherited interests
Sale of 239.99 acres of Crow allotment No. 28SA approved
Sale of 480 acres of Crow allotment No. 28SA approved
Rentals on approximately 1,283 acres kept paid up in advance for five years under competent leases

fee_patents.allottee_name: wife of Frank Medicine Horse, Jr. (mother's estate portion)

correspondence.subject: Status of patent in fee application / petition for sale by Rose Bends, Crow allottee No. 1001
correspondence.action_requested: Explanation of why Mrs. Bends' request was not approved
correspondence.outcome: Explained that petition for sale was denied due to recent large sales by husband William Bends; Mrs. Bends advised of appeal rights

correspondence.recipient: B. C. Schrock / Crow Indian Agency
correspondence.subject: Request for information on status of patent in fee application by Rose Bends
correspondence.action_requested: Information on status of patent in fee application
correspondence.outcome: Agency responded May 17 clarifying it was a petition for sale, not patent in fee, and explaining denial

correspondence.subject: Return of patent in fee applications and questionnaire
correspondence.action_requested: Old Bull to file applications directly with Crow Agency Office Superintendent
correspondence.outcome: Applications returned; Old Bull advised to file with Agency and notify Senator when filed

correspondence.sender_title: Area Director, BIA Billings Area Office
correspondence.subject: Report on Pete Old Bull's petition for supervised land sale which was disapproved
correspondence.action_requested: Response to Senator Murray's request of April 4
correspondence.outcome: Explained Old Bull's circumstances and denial; advised Old Bull could appeal to Commissioner of Indian Affairs

correspondence.recipient_title: Area Director, BIA Billings Area Office
correspondence.subject: Request for report regarding Pete Old Bull's letter about petition for supervised land sale
correspondence.action_requested: Report on status of Old Bull's petition
correspondence.outcome: Cooper responded April 13 with full report

correspondence.sender_title: Acting Area Director, BIA Billings Area Office
correspondence.subject: Status of sale of Crow allotment No. 28SA of Susan Farwell Glenn, deceased
correspondence.action_requested: Response to Senator Murray's inquiry of February 8 enclosing letter from Percy H. Glenn
correspondence.outcome: Reported approval of both sales; patents requested from BLM

correspondence.subject: Request to expedite patent for mother's land sale proceeds needed for father's nursing home care
correspondence.action_requested: Expedite issuance of patent for Susan Farwell Glenn's land sale
correspondence.outcome: Senator Murray wrote to Area Director; patents eventually issued March 3 and March 8

correspondence.subject: Reply to Glenn's letter of February 4 regarding mother's land sale
correspondence.action_requested: Advised Glenn that matter not yet in Washington office; writing to Area Director at Billings
correspondence.outcome: Murray wrote to Area Director Cooper same day

correspondence.recipient_title: Area Director, BIA Billings Area Office
correspondence.subject: Request to check into Susan Farwell Glenn land patent matter and advise of status
correspondence.action_requested: Check into matter, advise of present status, urge prompt action
correspondence.outcome: Cooper's office responded February 14 with full status report

correspondence.subject: Telegram advising of patent numbers issued to Fraser and Roberts
correspondence.action_requested: Informational - patent numbers conveyed
correspondence.outcome: Glenn notified of Patent No. 1150460 (Fraser, March 3) and Patent No. 1150457 (Roberts, March 8)

correspondence.subject: Patents issued on allotments for Frank Medicine Horse, Jr. and Frederick P. Knows Gun
correspondence.action_requested: Response to Murray's communication of March 4
correspondence.outcome: Reported patent numbers and dates; advised patentees to consult Area Director if not yet received

correspondence.subject: Patents issued on allotments for Frank Medicine Horse, Jr. and Frederick P. Knows Gun (duplicate/follow-up letter)
correspondence.action_requested: Response to Murray's communication of March 4
correspondence.outcome: Same information as March 13 letter regarding patent numbers and allotments

correspondence.subject: Status of patent in fee for heirs of father's estate; wife's application
correspondence.action_requested: Advising Medicine Horse of contact with Indian Bureau; will advise when information received
correspondence.outcome: Murray also took up wife's application for patent in fee for portion of her mother's estate

correspondence.sender_title: Area Director, BIA Billings Area Office
correspondence.subject: Status of Lester Jefferson's application for patent in fee for land inherited from father, Crow allottee No. 799
correspondence.action_requested: Response to Murray's inquiry of December 10
correspondence.outcome: Reported no recent application received; Jefferson's program for expenditure of funds not sound enough; family depends on lease income

correspondence.subject: Suggestions for obtaining patent in fee without special legislation
correspondence.action_requested: Suggested Jefferson discuss revised expenditure program with Superintendent or appeal March 1953 denial
correspondence.outcome: Murray advised against private bill route; suggested administrative procedures first

correspondence.subject: Issuance of patent in fee to Lester Jefferson for lands inherited from father, Crow allottee No. 799
correspondence.action_requested: Requesting Area Director to inform Murray of present status of Jefferson's application
correspondence.outcome: Area Director Cooper responded January 5, 1955

correspondence.sender_title: Area Director, BIA Billings Area Office
correspondence.subject: Lester Jefferson patent in fee application for land inherited from father, Crow allottee No. 799
correspondence.action_requested: Reference to Commissioner's letter of December 31, 1954; advising Superintendent Lippert of Murray's interest
correspondence.outcome: Cooper advising Superintendent Lippert to give Jefferson's application special attention

correspondence.subject: Acknowledgment of Jefferson's letter of November 22 regarding patent in fee status
correspondence.action_requested: Murray advising Jefferson he had contacted BIA and BLM; difficulty locating application for allotment No. 799
correspondence.outcome: Murray promised to keep Jefferson advised of developments

correspondence.sender_title: Chief Clerk, office of Senator Murray
correspondence.subject: Bureau of Indian Affairs policy of forcing patent on complete allotment holdings rather than partial
correspondence.action_requested: Information to be forwarded to Mr. Shaw; Commissioner to appear before Indian Affairs Subcommittee
correspondence.outcome: Promised to send information after Commissioner's appearance before subcommittee

correspondence.sender: unnamed letter writer (Percy H. Glenn or family member)
correspondence.subject: Thanks for getting patent through quickly; receipt of money from land sale
correspondence.action_requested: None - thank you letter
correspondence.outcome: Expressed gratitude for Senator Murray's efforts

legislative_actions.outcome: Commissioner of Indian Affairs scheduled to appear before Indian Affairs Subcommittee to explain Bureau policies on 'all or nothing' patenting; no private bill introduced per Murray's advice to Jefferson
